creator: stephenfisher@outlook.com
text:
When I start system-config-printer from the command line (since it doesn't work from Gnome's system menu), I get these messages followed by the application stopping:

** (process:61420): WARNING **: Trying to register gtype 'GMountMountFlags' as enum when in fact it is of type 'GFlags'

** (process:61420): WARNING **: Trying to register gtype 'GDriveStartFlags' as enum when in fact it is of type 'GFlags'

** (process:61420): WARNING **: Trying to register gtype 'GSocketMsgFlags' as enum when in fact it is of type 'GFlags'
GLib (gthread-posix.c): Unexpected error from C library during 'pthread_mutex_unlock': Operation not permitted.  Aborting.

How-To-Repeat: Run system-config-printer from the command line
